original: St Sixtus II begins his reign as Catholic Pope
Duke Lotharius of Supplinburg elected king of Germany
European leaders outlaw the crossbow, intending to end war for all time
Beginning of the Battle of Lake Poyang; two Chinese rebel leaders, Chen Youliang and Zhu Yuanzhang, clash in one of the largest naval battles in history during the Mongol-led Yuan Dynasty
Pietro Barbo elected to succeed Pope Pius II as Pope Paul II
Jewish community of Neutitschlin, Moravia, expelled
Ram Das becomes the 4th Sikh Guru
Dutch and Indians sign peace treaty in New Amsterdam (NY)
Leopold I, Spain, the Netherlands, and the Lutherans form an anti-French alliance
Russian and Swedish sign Treaty of Nystad, ending North Sea War
Battle of Gross-Jägersdorf: Russian army defeats Prussia (August 19 in the old system)
Continental Army evacuates Long Island and falls back to Manhattan, NYC
HMS Pandora sinks after running aground on a reef the previous day on her return from her search for the Bounty and the mutineers who had taken her
Batavian fleet surrenders to British navy during Anglo-Russian invasion of Holland
Gabriel Prosser leads a slave rebellion in Richmond, Virginia
Battle of Kulm: French forces defeated by Austrian-Prussian-Russian alliance
City of Melbourne, Australia, is founded
City of Houston is founded by Augustus Chapman Allen and John Kirby Allen
African Americans participate in a national political convention (Liberty Party) for the first time
Honolulu, Hawaii, becomes a city
First British tram begins operating in Birkenhead
John Frémont issues proclamation freeing slaves of Missouri rebels
Battle of Altamont: Confederates defeat Union forces in Tennessee
Austrian explorers Julius von Payer and Karl Weyprecht discover the archipelago of Franz Josef Land in the Arctic Ocean
13,000 meteors are seen in one hour near the Andromeda Galaxy, the closest major galaxy to the Milky Way at 2.537 million light-years from Earth
Lord Walsingham kills 1,070 grouse in a single day
Shipwreck of the "The Western Reserve" on Lake Superior during bad weather; 27 people drown with only one survivor
British explorer Frederick Lugard begins his expedition to Niger
Belgium begins compulsory Roman Catholic education
Eight provinces in the Philippines are declared under martial law by Spanish Governor General Ramon Blanco, including the provinces of Batangas, Rizal, Cavite, and Nueva Ecija
The town of Ambiky is captured by France from Menabe in Madagascar
Last 2,000 British prisoners in Nooitgedagt, South Africa, freed
English engineer Hubert Cecil Booth patents the powered vacuum cleaner
American runner Thomas Hicks wins the marathon gold medal with a time of 3:28:53 over a 40 km course at the St. Louis Olympics
Pogoro/Ngindo attack Fort Mahenge in German East Africa
Hal Chase becomes the first New York Yankee to hit three triples in a game
New York Highlander Joe Doyle debuts, pitching back-to-back shutouts

Burgess Shale fossil site, one of the most diverse and best-preserved in the world, is discovered by Charles Doolittle Walcott in the Canadian Rocky Mountains (now British Columbia's Yoho National Park)
MLB New York Highlanders' Tom Hughes pitches 9-1/3 no-hit innings but loses to Cleveland 5-0 in 11 innings; the 1991 rule change removes credit for a no-hitter
St. Louis Browns' Earl Hamilton no-hits the Detroit Tigers, 5-1
Phillies lead Giants 8-6 in the top of the 9th, fans in the bleachers try to distract the Giants, umpire forfeits the game to the Giants, later overruled
First German plane bombs Paris, killing two
Dutch Leonard of the Boston Red Sox no-hits the St Louis Browns 4-0
Czechoslovakia forms an independent republic
Ernst Toller's "Die Wandlung" premieres in Berlin
Babe Ruthis thrown out of a game for the fifth time in 1922
Sixth Iron Pilgrimage at Diksmuide, Belgium
Air France forms from five French airlines
General Reijnders is appointed supreme commander of the Dutch army
New York Yankee Atley Donald pitches a baseball at a record 94.7 mph (152 km/h)
Poland mobilizes for war
Sixth NFL Chicago All-Star Game: NY Giants 9, All-Stars 0 (81,456 attendees)
St. Louis Cardinal Lon Warneke no-hits Cincinnati Reds, 2-0
Nazi Germany annexes Luxembourg
11th NFL Chicago All-Star Game: Chicago Bears 24, All-Stars 21 (48,769 attendees)
Soviet troops enter Bucharest, Romania
12th NFL Chicago All-Star Game: Green Bay 19, All-Stars 7 (92,753 attendees)
Hong Kong is liberated from Japanese control
Roly Jenkins (Worcs vs. Surrey) takes his second hat-trick of the match
WTVN (now WSYX) TV channel 6 in Columbus, OH (ABC) begins broadcasting
The US and the Philippines sign a mutual defense pact
BC Lions play their franchise's first CFL game in Vancouver and lose to the Saskatchewan Roughriders 17-0
Hurricane Carol kills 68 on the US East Coast
Lake Pontchartrain Causeway opens in Louisiana, the longest continuous bridge in the world
USSR conducts a nuclear atmospheric test
White mob prevents enrollment of Black students at Mansfield High School, Texas
US performs a nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
The US conducts a nuclear test over the South Atlantic Ocean as part of Operation Argus to study the Christofilos effect, a theoretical defensive shield to cloud Soviet radar
Boston second baseman Pete Runnels goes 6-for-7
East Germany imposes a partial blockade on West Berlin
US women's 4 x 100 m medley relay team swims a world record of 4:41.1 to beat Australia and win the gold medal at the Rome Olympics; Lynn Burke, Patty Kempner, Carolyn Schuler, and Chris von Saltza
James Benton Parsons is confirmed as the first African American judge of a US District Court
Last Spanish troops leave Morocco
OrioleJack Fisherwalks 12 LA Angels in a 9-inning game
USSR announces it will resume nuclear testing
Japan conducts a test of the NAMC YS-11, its first aircraft since the war and its only successful commercial aircraft before or after the war
Compact audio cassette is first introduced by Philips at the Radio Exhibition in Berlin, Germany

Hotline communication link between the Pentagon in Washington, D.C., and the Kremlin in Moscow is installed. Often known as the "red telephone," no phones are ever used, relying instead on Teletype equipment, fax machines, and later, secure email.
Electric designer Norman Manley records back-to-back holes-in-one on the 7th and 8th holes at Del Valle in Saugus, California, setting a Guinness World Record
Section of Allalin Glacier wipes out construction site at Mattmark Dam near Saas-Fee, Switzerland
American medical drama series "Dr. Kildare," starringRichard Chamberlainand Raymond Massey, ends a five-season run on NBC-TV
The Beatles release the single "Hey Jude," their first record on their Apple label in the UK
American Steve Melnyk wins the 69th US Golf Amateur Championship
Racial disturbances in Fort Lauderdale, Florida

Three-day second Annual Sky River Rock Festival opens in Tenino, Washington, with 25,000 attendees, featuring performers such as James Cotton, Country Joe and the Fish, Flying Burrito Brothers, Dan Hicks and His Hot Licks, Mississippi Fred McDowell,Steve MillerBand, and Sons of Champlin

Three-day Texas International Pop Festival opens in Lewisville, Texas, with 125,000 attendees, featuring performers such as Chicago Transit Authority, Delaney and Bonnie and Friends, Grand Funk Railroad,Janis Joplin, B.B. King, Led Zeppelin, Nazz, Sam and Dave, Sly and the Family Stone, and Johnny Winter
WNPI TV channel 18 in Norwood, NY (PBS) begins broadcasting

American women's 4 x 100m freestyle relay team of Shirley Babashoff, Jane Barkman, Jenny Kemp, and Sandy Neilson beats East Germany by just 0.36 seconds to win gold at the Munich Olympics with a world record time of 3:55.19
Australian teenage swimmerShane Gouldwins her second of three gold medals in a world record time of 4:19.04 in the 400 m freestyle at the Munich Olympics
Japanese breaststroker Nobutaka Taguchi beats Americans Tom Bruce and John Hencken to win the 100 m gold medal in a world record time of 1:04.94 at the Munich Olympic Games
Slalom canoeing is introduced at the Munich Olympics, with East Germany winning all four events
Express train runs at full speed into a rail yard in Zagreb, Yugoslavia, killing 153
Launch of the first Dutch satellite, ANS, from Vandenberg
US performs a nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
KTW-AM in Seattle, Washington changes call letters to KYAC (now KKFX)
Turks and Caicos Islands adopts a constitution
First recorded occurrence of a comet hitting the Sun releases energy equal to one million hydrogen bombs
Hurricane David kills 1,200 in Florida, Dominica, and the Dominican Republic
8th NASA Space Shuttle Mission: Challenger 3 launches for a 6-day excursion
Guion Bluford becomes the first African American astronaut in space

Philadelphia TV station WKBS-TV (channel 48) ends broadcasting after surrendering its license to the FCC, the first to do so in at least 10 years, as parent company Field Enterprises Inc. is unable to find a buyer
12th NASA Space Shuttle Mission (41D): Discovery 1 launches for a 6-day excursion
Red Sox's Jim Rice grounds into a record 33rd double play en route to 36
Sotheby's in London begins a two-day auction of rock memorabilla
Gelindo Bordin wins the Stuttgart marathon in 2:10:54
Soviet authorities arrest US News & World Report journalist Nicholas Daniloff
Yuriy Sedykh of the Soviet Union sets a new world hammer throw record of 86.74 m (284 ft 6 in) in Stuttgart, Germany
Ben Johnson of Canada runs the 100 m in a world record time of 9.83 seconds
Billy Mayfair wins the 87th US Golf Amateur Championship
Kirby Puckettgoes 6-for-6 with two home runs in Minnesota's 10-6 win over Milwaukee
Knuckleballer Charlie Hough is on the mound, and Rangers catcher Geno Petralli ties the major league record by allowing six passed balls
Stefka Kostadinova of Bulgaria sets the women's high jump record at 2.09 m (6 ft 10½ in), one of the longest-standing records in athletics
Street Fighter, the first arcade fighting game to introduce special attacks, designed byTakashi Nishiyama, is released
Yves Pol of France runs a marathon backward in 3:57:57
France performs a nuclear test
Kent Tekulve is the second pitcher in the majors to appear in 1,000 games
Tatarstan announces its sovereignty with the Declaration of State Sovereignty of the Tatar Soviet Socialist Republic
Dan O'Brien sets a US decathlon record with 8,812 points
Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam abduct Sri Lankan Tamil language poet Chelvy Thiyagarajah and later execute her
Mike Powell of the US sets a long jump record of 29 ft 4½ in (8.95 m)
David Jewitt and Jane Luu discover the object "1992 QB1" 4.4 billion kilometers from the Sun
Gund Arena in Cleveland, Ohio, opens
Cable News Network joins the internet
NATO launches the air campaign Operation Deliberate Force against Bosnian Serb forces
Tigers teammates Lou Whitaker and Alan Trammell play in their 1,914th game together, tying the American League record
First WNBA Championship, Compaq Center, Houston: Top-seeded Houston Comets defeat NY Liberty 65-51 to win inaugural title; MVP: Houston Comets guard Cynthia Cooper
People of East Timor vote for independence from Indonesia in a referendum
MLB Atlanta Braves pitcherGreg Madduxwins his 330th career game
A blast in the Xiaojiawan coal mine, China, kills 26 miners, with 21 missing
Cholera outbreak kills 229 people in Sierra Leone
Fifteen people are killed by a liquid ammonia leak at a cold storage plant in Shanghai, China
Frederick Forsyth, author of thriller novel "The Day of the Jackal", reveals that he worked for Britain's MI6 Intelligence Service for more than 20 years
Authorities say floods across Bangladesh, Nepal, and India have killed more than 1,200 people and damaged 697,000 houses
Argentina's central bank raises interest rates to 60% in an attempt to stabilize the peso
Outlook for the Great Barrier Reef is downgraded to very poor according to official Australian report
Second interstellar comet detected by Ukrainian amateur astronomer Gennady Borisov, later named 2I/Borisov
Global cases of COVID-19 surpass 25 million, with the death toll at 843,000
India reports world's highest single-day increase in COVID-19 cases at 78,761
Algeria becomes the last country to stop selling leaded petrol, ending 99 years of gasoline use worldwide, saving 1.2 million lives a year
America ends its longest war of 20 years in Afghanistan as the last military evacuation plane flies out of Kabul
China restricts online gaming for those under 18 to one hour on Fridays, weekends, and holidays and orders companies to enforce this
In Jackson, Mississippi, the city's largest water treatment plant fails, leaving 150,000 people without safe running water and closing schools and businesses
Ukraine begins a counterattack against Russia in the southern Kherson region
Gabonese military leaders seize power in a coup, placing President Ali Bongo Ondimba under house arrest in Libreville
Hurricane Idalia makes landfall as a Category 3 storm in Florida's Big Bend, killing at least two people
Largest bombardment of Ukrainian capital Kyiv for months by Russia leaves two dead as Ukraine also bombards Russian territories with drones
More than 100 UK school buildings at risk of collapse are told to close because of reinforced autoclaved aerated concrete days before the new school year

The MacLean brothers, Ewan (32), Jamie (31), and Lachlan (27) of Scotland arrive in Cairns, Australia in a rowboat from Lima, Peru, setting new record for non-stop unsupported crossing of the Pacific Ocean (139 days, 5 hours, 52 minutes)
Tokugawa Ieyasuenters Edo Castle (Traditional Japanese date: August 1, 1590)
Mughal EmperorAurangzebhas his brother and competitor for the throne, Dara Shikoh, executed on religious grounds and his body paraded through the streets of Delhi (Gregorian calendar: September 9)
William Pennleaves England to sail to the New World
George Frideric Handelcompletes oratorio "Jephtha"

Thomas Jeffersonresponds toBenjamin Banneker's letter on the issue of slavery, writing that "nobody wishes more ardently to see a good system commenced for raising the condition" of Blacks "to what it ought to be"
PresidentBenjamin Harrisonsigns the first US law requiring inspection of meat products
Detroit Tigers future Baseball Hall of Fame center fielderTy Cobbmakes his MLB debut, doubling offJack Chesbroin a 5-3 win over the New York Highlanders at Bennett Park in Detroit

Battle ofTannenberg(WWI) in East Prussia ends in the destruction of the Russian Second Army, with 122,000-170,000 soldiers killed, injured, or captured by the German 8th Army led byPaul von HindenburgandErich Ludendorff
Fanya Kaplan attempts but fails to assassinateLenin, the new leader of Soviet Russia
The Rentenmark is replaced by the Reichsmark as legal tender in Germany, duringHans Luther's tenure as Minister of Finance.
Jawaharlal Nehrusubmits theNehruReport, officially requesting independence for India and outlining a federal constitution with reserved seats for minorities
Hermann Göringis elected President of the German Reichstag
Portuguese dictatorAntónio de Oliveira Salazarforms the secret police (PIDE)
Joe Louisbeats Tommy Farr in 15 rounds for the heavyweight boxing title
Isoroku Yamamotois appointed Commander-in-Chief of the Japanese Imperial Navy Combined Fleet
Winston Churchillapproves a nuclear program (Tube Alloys), becoming the first national leader to do so
Dmitri Shostakovichcompletes his 9th Symphony
WWII: US GeneralDouglas MacArthurlands in Japan
Casey Stengelannounces his retirement from managing the New York Mets after a 56-year career in professional baseball
US Senate confirmsThurgood Marshallas the first African American justice to serve on the Supreme Court
Three-day New Orleans Pop Festival opens with 25,000 attendees, featuring performers such as Spiral Starecase, T. Rex, The Byrds,Janis Joplin, Grateful Dead, Chicago,Dr. John, and Santana
AmidstOlga Korbutmania at the Munich Olympics, Soviet teammate Ludmilla Tourischeva wins the gymnastics individual all-around competition, her second gold medal of the Games
John LennonandYoko Ono's "One to One" benefit shows (matinee and evening) for the Willowbrook School, are held at Madison Square Garden, New York City they become his final full concert performances
Drummer Danny Seiwell quits rock band Wings, which he co-founded withPaul McCartney
Wang Hongwentakes office as Vice Chairman of the Chinese Communist Party under Mao Zedong
Tom Brokawstarts as news anchor of NBC's "The Today Show"
Bill Shoemakerbecomes the first jockey to win a $1 million thoroughbred horse race as John Henry wins the inaugural Arlington Million by a nose over The Bart
PLO leaderYasser Arafatleaves Beirut
Octavia E. Butlerwins her first Hugo award for Best Short Story with the science fiction story "Speech Sounds"
Philip Glass's Symphony No. 1 (Low), based loosely onDavid Bowie's 1977 album "Low," premieres with the Junge Deutsche Kammerphilharmonie conducted by Dennis Russell Davies in Munich, Germany
"Late Show withDavid Letterman" debuts on CBS from the Ed Sullivan Theater in NYC;Bill Murrayis the first guest, andBilly Joelperforms
Hassan IIMosque opens in Casablanca, the second-largest mosque in the world
Former Malaysian Prime MinisterMahathir bin Mohamadcalls for the removal of then Prime MinisterNajib Razakduring the second day of street protests
Brazilian court blocks PresidentMichel Temerfrom abolishing Renca, which would open parts of the Amazon to mining
Late authorTerry Pratchett's unfinished works are destroyed by a steamroller as per his instructions
Ugandan pop star and MPBobi Wineis arrested again as he attempts to leave the country
Co-founder and CEO of Twitter,Jack Dorsey, has his Twitter account hacked; racist comments are posted in his name
Brazil blocksElon Musk's social network X after it misses a court-imposed deadline

American singerCyndi Lauperwraps up her "Girls Just Wanna Have Fun Farewell Tour" at the Hollywood Bowl in Los Angeles, California; concert includes guest appearances by Cher,Joni Mitchell, SZA, and John Legend
Second Battle of Bull Run in Manassas, Virginia, ends with a Confederate victory over Union forces
150,000,000th visitor to the Eiffel Tower
NASA spacecraft Voyager 2 crosses the termination shock, where solar and interstellar winds meet (following Voyager 1 in 2004)
Hurricane Irma forms near the Cape Verde Islands and goes on to become a Category 5 hurricane, killing at least 102
